content:
Most people pay attention when new blockchain infrastructure launches.
Far fewer pay attention when older infrastructure is retired.
Yet bridge shutdowns are just as important for users.
The upcoming closure of the legacy Toncoin and Token Bridge is a reminder that blockchain infrastructure continues to evolve.
As newer cross-chain solutions emerge, older systems eventually reach the end of their lifecycle, making it essential for users to review where their assets are held.
This isn’t just about moving tokens before a deadline.
It’s about understanding how DeFi ecosystems mature. Legacy bridges helped connect TON with other networks during an earlier phase of growth. Today, newer infrastructure is focusing on improving security, execution, and user experience while reducing reliance on older architectures.
For anyone who previously used the Toncoin and Token Bridge, now is a good time to check whether any wrapped assets or j-tokens remain on the old bridge. Acting before the shutdown helps ensure those assets remain accessible.
The broader lesson is simple: in crypto, maintaining your portfolio isn’t only about tracking prices. It’s also about staying informed as the infrastructure behind the ecosystem continues to evolve.
Bridge assets before the shutdown: https://bridge-v3.ton.org/
#STON #TON
